etymology.description: 楊(楊) (yáng, "poplar") refers to a type of tree commonly used in personal names. The character 斌(斌) (bīn, "refined; cultivated") conveys qualities of elegance or refinement, typically used in names to suggest cultured or scholarly attributes.
etymology.interpretation: 楊(楊) (yáng, "poplar") likely serves as a family name and doesn't directly contribute meaning beyond identification. 斌(斌) (bīn, "refined; cultivated") implies traits of sophistication and culture. Most names in Chinese culture include characters that represent positive attributes, suggesting that the person is or will be erudite or of noble character.

examples.chinese: 杨斌是我最好的朋友(楊斌是我最好的朋友)。
examples.pinyin: Yáng Bīn shì wǒ zuì hǎo de péngyǒu.
examples.english: Yang Bin is my best friend.

examples.chinese: 我昨天和杨斌一起去看电影(我昨天和楊斌一起去看電影)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zuótiān hé Yáng Bīn yīqǐ qù kàn diànyǐng.
examples.english: I went to watch a movie with Yang Bin yesterday.

examples.chinese: 杨斌喜欢旅行(楊斌喜歡旅行)，每年都会去不同的国家(每年都會去不同的國家)。
examples.pinyin: Yáng Bīn xǐhuān lǚxíng, měi nián dū huì qù bùtóng de guójiā.
examples.english: Yang Bin enjoys traveling and visits different countries every year.
